reviewQueue.name: Advantageous Attack
reviewQueue.description: When you make a successful attack against an enemy who has not yet acted in combat, you add your full Heroic Level to damage rolls.
reviewQueue.whyUnsureOrNeedsReview: The catalog metadata describes an attack bonus keyed to target speed, but the source rule is a damage rider against an enemy who has not yet acted. This is the exact kind of wrong-shape implementation that must not count as implemented.
reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match damage_result_timing_hook, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?

reviewQueue.name: Advantageous Cover
reviewQueue.description: When you have Cover, you take no damage from Area Attacks, even if the attack roll exceeds your Reflex Defense.
reviewQueue.whyUnsureOrNeedsReview: Metadata preserves the cover context, but no proven runtime hook reduces or alters area-attack damage using cover state. Correct implementation needs area attack + cover result context, not static defense.
reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match cover_area_damage_rule, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?

reviewQueue.name: Angled Throw
reviewQueue.description: When throwing a Grenade or grenadelike weapon, you can attempt to bounce it off a wall or other surface close to your target. If you attack roll exceeds a Reflex Defense of 15, you ignore Cover and Improved Cover (but not Total Cover) with your attack.
reviewQueue.whyUnsureOrNeedsReview: Attack-option metadata exists, but the runtime must verify grenade/grenadelike thrown attack, bounce permissibility, Reflex 15 threshold, and cover vs total cover distinction. Current metadata is not enough for fully correct automation.
reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match grenade_cover_bypass_option, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?

reviewQueue.name: Bad Feeling
reviewQueue.description: You can always take a Move Action during a Surprise Round, even if you are Surprised. If you are not Surprised, you can take this Move Action in addition to any other Actions you are normally allowed to take in the Surprise Round.
reviewQueue.whyUnsureOrNeedsReview: Action metadata exists, but a real surprise-round action economy hook is not proven. Correct implementation grants/permits a Move Action during surprise rounds without altering ordinary action totals.
reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match surprise_round_action_capability, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?

reviewQueue.name: Blaster Barrage
reviewQueue.description: When you make an attack with a weapon set on Autofire that deals damage to at least one target within the designated area, you grant a +2 circumstance bonus on any of your allies' Autofire attacks made against that same target until the beginning of your next turn.
reviewQueue.whyUnsureOrNeedsReview: Autofire rider metadata exists, but full correctness requires an autofire result hook and ally participation context. It should not be a passive attack bonus.
reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match autofire_ally_bonus_context, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?

reviewQueue.name: Controlled Rage
reviewQueue.description: You enter a Rage as a Free Action. Your Rage ends 1 round after you declare it is finished.
reviewQueue.whyUnsureOrNeedsReview: Requires free-action rage entry/end control. The current metadata does not prove a rage-state runtime flow that changes activation/ending action cost.
reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match rage_action_state_rule, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?

reviewQueue.name: Crossfire
reviewQueue.description: If you miss when making a ranged attack against a target that has Soft Cover (that is, Cover provided by another character, creature, or Droid), you can immediately make an attack roll (with the same weapon and at the same attack bonus) against the target that is providing the Soft Cover. You can only use this Feat once per round.
reviewQueue.whyUnsureOrNeedsReview: Metadata exists, but full correctness requires detecting a missed ranged attack due to soft cover and identifying the soft-cover provider for the follow-up attack.
reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match ranged_miss_soft_cover_followup, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?

reviewQueue.name: Crush
reviewQueue.description: If you successfully Pin an opponent with a Grapple attack (see Pin Feat for more details), you can immediately deal bludgeoning damage to it equal to your Unarmed damage or claw damage, whichever is greater.
reviewQueue.whyUnsureOrNeedsReview: Metadata/source classification exists, but a correct implementation needs a successful Pin/grapple state and damage rider. It is not a Force feat and should not become static damage.
reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match grapple_pin_damage_rider, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?

reviewQueue.name: Focused Rage
reviewQueue.description: While Raging, you can use Skills that require patience and concentration, at a -5 penalty.
reviewQueue.whyUnsureOrNeedsReview: Requires allowing patience/concentration-type skill usage while raging at the correct contextual penalty. No proven rage skill-permission hook exists.
reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match rage_skill_permission, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?

reviewQueue.name: Forceful Blast
reviewQueue.description: When you damage a Large or smaller creature with a Grenade or Thermal Detonator, compare the result of your attack roll to the target's Fortitude Defense. If your result equals or exceeds the target's Fortitude Defense, you can move the target 1 square in any direction as a Free Action. If the Grenade deals damage to multiple eligible creatures, you can use this benefit against all of them. You can't move a target that's being Grabbed or Grappled, and you can't move a target into a solid object or into another creature's Fighting Space.
reviewQueue.whyUnsureOrNeedsReview: Metadata identifies a grenade/thermal detonator rider, but full correctness requires a hit-result rider with push/prone/forced-movement handling. It is not a Force feat despite the name.
reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match grenade_hit_rider, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?

reviewQueue.name: Forceful Grip
reviewQueue.description: You gain a +2 bonus on Use the Force checks made to activate Force Grip.
reviewQueue.whyUnsureOrNeedsReview: Skill bonus metadata exists, but full correctness requires Force power activation context that applies +2 only to Force Grip activation checks and never to generic Use the Force.
reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match scoped_force_power_activation_bonus, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?

reviewQueue.name: Forceful Recovery
reviewQueue.description: Whenever you catch a Second Wind, choose one expended Force Power and return that power to your Force Power Suite.
reviewQueue.whyUnsureOrNeedsReview: Requires a second-wind hook and player/GM choice of one expended Force power to return to the suite. No proven hook exists.
reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match second_wind_force_power_recovery, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?

reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match scoped_force_power_activation_bonus, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?

reviewQueue.name: Forceful Vitality
reviewQueue.description: You gain a +2 bonus on Endurance checks and may reroll a failed Endurance check once per encounter.
reviewQueue.whyUnsureOrNeedsReview: Skill bonus/reroll metadata exists and the skill feat resolver can read those rule families, but full correctness requires once-per-encounter failed Endurance reroll usage tracking.
reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match skill_bonus_and_encounter_reroll, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?

reviewQueue.name: Forceful Weapon
reviewQueue.description: You gain a +2 bonus on Use the Force checks made to activate Battle Strike.
reviewQueue.whyUnsureOrNeedsReview: Skill bonus metadata exists, but full correctness requires activation context for Battle Strike only.
reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match scoped_force_power_activation_bonus, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?

reviewQueue.name: Forceful Will
reviewQueue.description: You gain a +2 bonus on Will Defense against mind-affecting effects and may reroll a failed Will Defense check once per encounter.
reviewQueue.whyUnsureOrNeedsReview: Conditional Will metadata exists, but full correctness requires mind-affecting context and once-per-encounter failed Will reroll handling. It must not become always-on Will Defense.
reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match conditional_defense_and_reroll, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?

reviewQueue.name: Improved Bantha Rush
reviewQueue.description: When making a Bantha Rush, you push your opponent a number of additional squares away from you equal to half your Strength modifier (Round down, minimum 2 squares pushed total).
reviewQueue.whyUnsureOrNeedsReview: Metadata exists, but full correctness requires Bantha Rush maneuver resolution and push-distance adjustment. It should not be static attack/damage math.
reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match combat_maneuver_distance_rule, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?

reviewQueue.name: Mighty Throw
reviewQueue.description: You can add your Strength modifier (in addition to your Dexterity modifier) to your ranged attack bonus when using Thrown Weapons (including Grenades and grenadelike weapons). Also, you can increase the length of each Range category by a number of squares equal to your Strength modifier.
reviewQueue.whyUnsureOrNeedsReview: The combat option resolver supports ATTACK_ABILITY_BONUS for thrown weapons, but full correctness also requires extending range categories by Strength modifier. Do not mark correct until range-band math is proven.
reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match thrown_weapon_attack_formula_and_range, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?

reviewQueue.name: Powerful Rage
reviewQueue.description: You gain a +4 bonus to Strength checks and Strength -based Skill Checks when Raging.
reviewQueue.whyUnsureOrNeedsReview: Requires rage-state damage handling. No proven runtime hook applies only while raging.
reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match rage_state_damage_bonus, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?

reviewQueue.name: Rapport
reviewQueue.description: When using the Aid Another Action, you grant an additional +2 insight bonus on Skill Checks and attack rolls to the character you are assisting. This bonus does not stack with any bonus provided by the Noble's Coordinate Talent.
reviewQueue.whyUnsureOrNeedsReview: Metadata can describe the support relationship, but correct automation needs Aid Another context, ally range/communication, and target action connection.
reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match aid_another_bonus_and_range, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?

reviewQueue.name: Strafe
reviewQueue.description: When you make an Autofire attack, instead of attacking a 2-square-by-2-square area, you may attack a line 1 square wide and 4 squares long.
reviewQueue.whyUnsureOrNeedsReview: Requires a specific autofire attack shape option. No proven action card/template hook exists.
reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match autofire_shape_attack_option, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?

reviewQueue.name: Swarm
reviewQueue.description: You gain a +1 circumstance bonus on melee attack rolls for each allied character adjacent to your target.
reviewQueue.whyUnsureOrNeedsReview: Metadata can express positioning context, but full correctness requires target adjacency/ally count checks in attack or damage context.
reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match target_position_context_bonus, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?

reviewQueue.name: Unstoppable Force
reviewQueue.description: You gain a +5 insight bonus to Fortitude Defense and Will Defense against any attack or effect requiring a Use the Force check.
reviewQueue.whyUnsureOrNeedsReview: Metadata/context summary may exist, but full correctness requires target-effect context for Force powers and should not become always-on defenses.
reviewQueue.accuracyQuestion: Does runtime behavior match conditional_force_defense_bonus, including required context/timing, rather than merely having metadata or a flat passive modifier?
